items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## đźŽŻ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` README following PR #58252, covering undocumented chat isolation, stale observability notes, outdated error descriptions, and missing metadata/ID constraints.

<details>
<summary>đź“‹ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (no version specified; triggered by PR #58252 / commit a43d15f)
**Affected API:** README.md â€” Key Concepts, Logging, Troubleshooting sections
**Scenarios:**
- Chat isolation key (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) is entirely undocumented; developers won't know about this security-relevant tenant-isolation feature
- `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` and auto-registered `HttpClient` instrumentation are not mentioned in the Logging section
- Troubleshooting section shows stale cancel/delete error shapes (404 vs 400 corrections from the PR)
- Metadata map limits (â‰¤16 keys, keys â‰¤64 chars, values â‰¤512 chars) are not documented
- Malformed response ID rejection (400) is not mentioned

**Root ask:** Update the README to reflect all behavioral changes introduced by PR #58252, including an implementation guide with exact text to add.
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź”Ž Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue filed by an automated workflow. No runtime bug or reproduction steps apply.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58252 (commit a43d15f by `@RaviPidaparthi`) to confirm the described changes are accurately captured in the issue body
2. Apply the README edits in the Implementation Guide (chat isolation subsection, expanded logging section, corrected troubleshooting bullets)
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` and `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` to verify no snippet or API surface changes are needed
4. No similar open documentation issues were found for this package in the repository
</details>

<details>
<summary>đźŹ·ď¸Ź Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` â€” `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` is a data-plane client SDK, not a management or provisioning library
- **Service:** `AI Agents` â€” Package lives in `sdk/agentserver/`; CODEOWNERS maps this path to the `%AI Agents` service label at line 85
- **Confidence:** High â€” The package name and service directory are explicitly stated in the issue body; both labels map unambiguously
</details>

<details>
<summary>đź‘Ą Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) â€” only entry for this service; matched on the "AI Agents" service label
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@ankitbko` `@RaviPidaparthi`
- **Routing action:** Added `Service Attention` label; `needs-team-attention` not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported); `@mention` sent via Step 6 routing comment
- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan found only one `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel entry at line 85 with no more specific multi-label override entries later in the file
</details>
